content.problemStatement:
You are given a binary string SSS.

In one operation, you can pick an index iii (1≤i<∣S∣)(1\le i \lt |S|)(1≤i<∣S∣) and swap the characters SiS_iSi​ and S(i+1)S_{(i+1)}S(i+1)​.

Find the **minimum** number of operations required, such that, the decimal representation of the final binary string is **divisible** by 333. If it is impossible to do so, print −1-1−1 instead.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of single line of input, containing a binary string SSS.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **minimum** number of operations required, such that, the decimal representation of the final binary string is **divisible** by 333. If it is impossible to do so, print −1-1−1 instead.
constraints: The sum of ∣S∣|S|∣S∣ over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053\cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There is no need to apply any operation since the decimal representation of 000000000000 is 000 which is divisible by 333.
Test case 222: It can be shown that we cannot make the decimal representation of the string divisible by 333 using any number of operations.
Test case 333: The decimal representation of 110011100111001 is 252525 . Using one operation, we pick i=2i = 2i=2 and swap S2S_2S2​ and S3S_3S3​. Thus, the string becomes 101011010110101, whose decimal representation is 212121, which is divisible by 333.
